title: campylanthus incanus
intro: Campylanthus incanus, also known as Afghan snowmass or Afghan beardtongue, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Afghanistan and surrounding areas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, Campylanthus incanus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
